<!-- GENERATED by pnpm adapters:generate — DO NOT EDIT -->

# Adapter gallery

An **adapter** teaches a chart library how to render chartlang emissions:
the runtime produces typed, JSON-safe emissions (candles, plots, drawings,
alerts) and the adapter translates them into draw calls on a specific chart
vendor's surface. One script, many charts.

There are two ways to get an adapter:

- **`npx @invinite-org/chartlang-cli add-adapter <id>`** — drops a complete,
  runnable, version-pinned copy of one of the full-surface example adapters
  below into your repo (offline; the templates are baked into the CLI).
- **`npx @invinite-org/chartlang-cli scaffold-adapter <name>`** — emits a blank
  starter package wired for conformance, for a library not in this gallery.

New to adapters? Read [Writing an adapter](./writing-an-adapter.md) for the
contract and [Conformance](./conformance.md) for the scenario suite. Every
adapter here declares the full surface (all 63 drawing kinds + every plot kind)
and is conformance-green.

## Comparison

| Adapter | Library | License | Render tech | Drawing surface | ~Bundle | Source |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| [Canvas 2D](#canvas2d) | none | MIT | HTML Canvas 2D context | Full (63 + all plots) | ~130 KB | [`examples/canvas2d-adapter/`](https://github.com/outraday-org/chartlang/tree/main/examples/canvas2d-adapter) |
| [ECharts](#echarts) | `echarts` | Apache-2.0 | Declarative graphic component | Full (63 + all plots) | ~64 KB | [`examples/echarts-adapter/`](https://github.com/outraday-org/chartlang/tree/main/examples/echarts-adapter) |
| [Konva](#konva) | `konva` | MIT | Scene-graph (Canvas) | Full (63 + all plots) | ~92 KB | [`examples/konva-adapter/`](https://github.com/outraday-org/chartlang/tree/main/examples/konva-adapter) |
| [Lightweight Charts](#lightweight-charts) | `lightweight-charts` | Apache-2.0 | Native series + series-primitive overlay | Full (63 + all plots) | ~64 KB | [`examples/lightweight-charts-adapter/`](https://github.com/outraday-org/chartlang/tree/main/examples/lightweight-charts-adapter) |
| [uPlot](#uplot) | `uplot` | MIT | Canvas draw-hooks | Full (63 + all plots) | ~56 KB | [`examples/uplot-adapter/`](https://github.com/outraday-org/chartlang/tree/main/examples/uplot-adapter) |

`~Bundle` is a hand-maintained estimate (see the [FAQ](#faq)).

## Canvas 2D {#canvas2d}

**Best for** — Zero-dependency reference; full control over every pixel.

- **Library:** none (zero external dependencies) · MIT
- **Render tech:** HTML Canvas 2D context (~130 KB)
- **Drawings:** paints the decomposed primitives onto a raw 2D canvas context.

Install:

```bash
npx @invinite-org/chartlang-cli add-adapter canvas2d
```

Source:
[folder](https://github.com/outraday-org/chartlang/tree/main/examples/canvas2d-adapter) ·
[README](https://github.com/outraday-org/chartlang/tree/main/examples/canvas2d-adapter#readme) ·
[conformance](https://github.com/outraday-org/chartlang/blob/main/examples/canvas2d-adapter/CONFORMANCE.md)

## ECharts {#echarts}

**Best for** — Huge install base; native candlesticks; declarative graphics.

- **Library:** `echarts` (^5) · Apache-2.0
- **Render tech:** Declarative graphic component (~64 KB)
- **Drawings:** maps each decomposed primitive to a declarative graphic element.

Install:

```bash
npx @invinite-org/chartlang-cli add-adapter echarts
```

Source:
[folder](https://github.com/outraday-org/chartlang/tree/main/examples/echarts-adapter) ·
[README](https://github.com/outraday-org/chartlang/tree/main/examples/echarts-adapter#readme) ·
[conformance](./conformance.md)

## Konva {#konva}

**Best for** — Retained-mode nodes; hit-testing for interactive drawings.

- **Library:** `konva` (^9) · MIT
- **Render tech:** Scene-graph (Canvas) (~92 KB)
- **Drawings:** maps each decomposed primitive to a retained-mode scene-graph node.

Install:

```bash
npx @invinite-org/chartlang-cli add-adapter konva
```

Source:
[folder](https://github.com/outraday-org/chartlang/tree/main/examples/konva-adapter) ·
[README](https://github.com/outraday-org/chartlang/tree/main/examples/konva-adapter#readme) ·
[conformance](./conformance.md)

## Lightweight Charts {#lightweight-charts}

**Best for** — Financial-native candles/axes/panes for free; tiny runtime.

- **Library:** `lightweight-charts` (^5) · Apache-2.0
- **Render tech:** Native series + series-primitive overlay (~64 KB)
- **Drawings:** renders series/panes natively and overlays drawings through a primitive's context.

Install:

```bash
npx @invinite-org/chartlang-cli add-adapter lightweight-charts
```

Source:
[folder](https://github.com/outraday-org/chartlang/tree/main/examples/lightweight-charts-adapter) ·
[README](https://github.com/outraday-org/chartlang/tree/main/examples/lightweight-charts-adapter#readme) ·
[conformance](./conformance.md)

## uPlot {#uplot}

**Best for** — Tiny + fast; immediate-mode draw hooks over raw context.

- **Library:** `uplot` (^1) · MIT
- **Render tech:** Canvas draw-hooks (~56 KB)
- **Drawings:** paints the decomposed primitives onto a raw 2D canvas context.

Install:

```bash
npx @invinite-org/chartlang-cli add-adapter uplot
```

Source:
[folder](https://github.com/outraday-org/chartlang/tree/main/examples/uplot-adapter) ·
[README](https://github.com/outraday-org/chartlang/tree/main/examples/uplot-adapter#readme) ·
[conformance](./conformance.md)

## Which should I choose?

- **Financial-native candles / axes / panes for free** → `lightweight-charts`.
- **Tiniest + fastest, immediate-mode draw hooks** → `uplot`.
- **Dashboards / huge ecosystem / declarative graphics** → `echarts`.
- **Interactive, custom, retained-mode scene-graph + hit-testing** → `konva`.
- **Zero-dependency reference, full control over every pixel** → `canvas2d`.

All five share one renderer-agnostic geometry layer
(`decomposeDrawing` in `@invinite-org/chartlang-adapter-kit`), so the drawing
math is identical — the choice is about your library, not the drawing surface.

## FAQ

**The examples aren't on npm — how do I add one?** They are private
(copy-only) example packages, not published. Use
`npx @invinite-org/chartlang-cli add-adapter <id>` (it bakes a version-pinned
copy into your repo) or copy the GitHub folder directly. `npm install` will not
find them.

**Why is the `~Bundle` size approximate?** `approxBundleKb` is a
hand-maintained field on the adapter registry, not auto-measured, so the
generated gallery + the `add-adapter --list` matrix stay deterministic and the
`adapters:gate` byte-diff never flaps on a measurement.

**Do drawings look pixel-identical across adapters?** The geometry is shared
via `decomposeDrawing`, but non-canvas adapters use a **linear price axis**
approximation for drawing projection (exact on a linear scale, approximate on a
log scale — a deferred follow-up). The shared geometry layer lives in
`@invinite-org/chartlang-adapter-kit`; canvas-family adapters also reuse its
`/canvas` `paintPrimitive` sink.

**Where's the committed conformance report?** Only the canvas2d reference
adapter ships a committed `CONFORMANCE.md`; the other four are run pass/fail by
`pnpm conformance` and link the [conformance docs](./conformance.md) instead.
